notes: Replaced bootstrap placeholder with an actual Beta-lane child ideation result.
Used the named authority surfaces plus current claim-context review without editing shared state.

evidence.summary: C45 already splits route execution into explicit native-sovereign versus leased-cognition modes, so the missing capability is not basic inference access but stronger internal substrate control.
C45 already compiles requests into sovereign execution plans, locks execution mode, and binds provenance, which means a literal parallel inference layer would duplicate route authority.
Leased cognition is explicitly outer-membrane only, which blocks any aggressive future that turns inference into a generic everywhere-reachable service plane.
C47 already defines the three-phase path from leased cognition to open-source native adaptation to fully native models, so model-supply evolution is already owned.
C47 explicitly says C45 chooses route execution while C47 supplies promoted native models, leaving a still-open gap for a dedicated internal serving and placement fabric between them.

C42 already governs continuation-ready and execution-primed work plus lawful runtime handoff, so any stronger inference substrate must remain subordinate to C42 and C23 rather than becoming ambient execution authority.
C36 is not a generic API gateway and keeps all external interaction membrane-typed, which means the aggressive future cannot be an externally framed generic model microservice layer.
Foundry and Enterprise exposure is bounded and explicitly forbids general-purpose inference or raw access into core surfaces.
C39 already defines delegated sampling request/result semantics, model provenance, and streamed progressive generation, so there is no missing forward protocol family for model calls.
C18 already assumes C45-governed leased cognition and financed C47 adaptation, so compute and model operations are already first-order economic surfaces.

C18 names sovereign compute reservation, temporary leased routes, and Forge adaptation/quarantine as explicit cost buckets, which supports elevating internal inference capacity into a real subsystem rather than leaving it implicit.
C22 already assumes bounded leased-provider wrappers plus local/native fallback, but still treats this mostly as an abstraction layer rather than a first-class governed inference fabric.

C22 already budgets sovereign compute, leased cognition, and Forge adaptation by wave, showing that the implementation program is ready for a sharper internal inference-capacity authority if the concept is promoted.

T-300 fixes Alternative C as the only forward communication authority, so any future concept that smells like an old generic service layer must be reframed as an internal Alternative C-native subsystem.

lane_strategy: Explore a materially different framing or topology for the current task.

model_audit.routing_basis: AAS4 ordinary FULL_PIPELINE / IDEATION default future-branch routing target.

objective: Explore the strongest capability-maximizing future for the current ideation problem. Apply that branch specifically to 'Independent Model Inference Microservice Layer'.
one_sentence_thesis: The literal microservice-layer idea is not forward-authority-valid under T-300, but the strongest capability-maximizing future still implies a new internal subsystem: a Sovereign Inference Fabric that governs pooled model capacity, placement, batching, backend provenance, and leased-to-native migration beneath C45 and fed by C47.

option_shapes.main_risk: Duplicates C45/C36/C39 authority and drifts back toward obsolete generic service-layer thinking.
option_shapes.main_upside: Easy to explain using conventional serving terminology.
option_shapes.name: Literal Independent Microservice Layer
option_shapes.summary: Keep the idea as a separately exposed model-serving layer with its own endpoints and routing contracts.

option_shapes.main_risk: Can become an overloaded C45 addendum that buries a major cross-cutting capability inside route wrappers.
option_shapes.main_upside: Requires less structural expansion and preserves current authority owners more directly.
option_shapes.name: C45/C47 Addendum Only
option_shapes.summary: Treat the idea as direct-spec refinements for backend pool semantics, native handoff, and cost-aware routing without creating a new subsystem.

option_shapes.main_risk: Needs very sharp boundaries so it does not absorb C45 route authority, C47 promotion authority, or C42 runtime authority.
option_shapes.main_upside: Maximizes capability: shared model pools, policy-aware placement, speculative and shadow execution, warm-state reuse, ensemble routing, and explicit substrate migration all become first-class internal powers.
option_shapes.name: Sovereign Inference Fabric
option_shapes.summary: Promote the objective into a new internal subsystem for inference-capacity governance that is consumed only through existing Alternative C execution and sampling surfaces.

aggressive_future_shape.name: Sovereign Inference Fabric
aggressive_future_shape.capability_thesis: Inference should become a governed internal capacity fabric, not a separate externally framed service layer.
core_capabilities: Pooled leased and native model backends with membrane-scoped admission.
Placement, batching, warm-state reuse, and queue arbitration across backend classes.
Shadow execution and ensemble routing for quality, safety, and promotion readiness.
Backend provenance disclosure that rides existing C39 sampling surfaces.
Explicit handoff boundaries so C42 and C23 remain the only execution-authority path.
Promotion-aware substrate migration that lets C45 retire leased routes as C47 native families mature.

authority_surface_disposition: C45: already owns route execution mode and bounded leased/native choice; aggressive future must sit beneath it, not beside it.
C42: already owns continuation and runtime handoff; aggressive future may optimize inference capacity but may not grant execution authority.
C47: already owns model ingestion, adaptation, quarantine, and promotion; aggressive future consumes promoted model families rather than replacing Forge authority.
C36: already owns exposure boundaries; aggressive future must stay internal and membrane-scoped, not public or ambient.
C39: already owns delegated sampling semantics and model provenance disclosure; aggressive future should extend backend facts through this path instead of inventing a new protocol family.
C18: already prices sovereign compute, leased cognition, and Forge throughput; aggressive future strengthens rather than bypasses that economics model.
C22: already sequences compute/model operations; aggressive future would require explicit rollout and capacity-governance milestones.
T-300: blocks revival of generic old-stack forward posture, forcing any valid future to be Alternative C-native and internal.
key_upsides: Separates lightweight agent scaling from heavyweight model-serving scaling without reopening old protocol assumptions.
Turns leased-to-native migration into an operationally governed substrate instead of a route-by-route improvisation.
Unlocks stronger capabilities than a thin wrapper: shared backend pools, speculative execution, shadow evaluation, and native promotion feedback loops.
Makes the economics already present in C18/C22 actionable through a real capacity-control authority.
key_risks: A sloppy design would duplicate C45 or C47 instead of complementing them.
If exposed externally, the subsystem would violate C36 and drift into doctrinally invalid generic inference-as-a-service behavior.
If it starts granting execution authority directly, it would collide with C42 and C23.
If kept too narrow, it collapses into a minor addendum and loses the capability-maximizing advantage that justifies new-subsystem status.
recommended_next_action: If the operator wants the strongest future, do not promote the idea as a literal microservice layer. Promote only a reframed FULL PIPELINE concept for an internal Sovereign Inference Fabric, then require downstream authority addenda in C45, C47, C42, C39, C18, and C22.
